title: blitum polymorphum var. humile
intro: Blitum polymorphum var. humile, also known as creeping Kenilworth or creeping blite, is a perennial flowering plant in the amaranth family, characterized by its sprawling habit and small, yellowish-green flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Blitum polymorphum var. humile are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be used topically as a poultice or salve to treat skin conditions and wounds.
